channel.title: Interfacing on Dressed in a Dress - The Sewing Journal
channel.description: Recent content in Interfacing on Dressed in a Dress - The Sewing Journal

item.title: What’s inside the fur coat? Part 2.
item.description:
In the previous post I wrote about the difference between interfacing and interlining. There I described the fabrics and techniques I used to give this coat shape and support.

And now my hands are burning, because it’s time to draw a pattern for the inner structure.

The Interfacing Patterns

I do not print the details of the inner structure in advance, so I place tracing paper on top of the pattern and draw new contours.

item.title: What’s inside the fur coat? Part 1.
item.description: As soon as snow covers the streets, I start to feel an uncontrollable desire to warm myself with at least one new sweater. Every time I finish a new knitted something, I swear to never touch the needles again. And every time, as soon as it becomes cold and dark, I start ordering yarn. I need a sweater, and a hat and a scarf, and a sweater, yes, it will be warmer. This year, the yarn did not help. A spontaneous trip to a fabric store for “just to have a look” ended up with me happily wrapped in a new shaggy piece of eco fur. After a month and a half, it will turn into a fur coat, which will make passers-by turn around.
